item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea makes sense, and is somewhat developed, however it suffers from weak tone and presentation. I'm also opposed to its Thaumiel classification, as in my mind it is not of sufficient operational necessity. This should just be a Safe-class that they occasionally use.

-1

item.title: Enjoyed it
item.content:encoded: I like the unique premise of the notebooks and their utility. The last srgement could be cross referenced with scp 2852 and scp 3004 which show Christianity as an anomolous force. Overall I enjoyed it man. Keep it ip

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not really my thing. I can see how others would like it, but it's just not up my alley.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's thaumiel because it's capable of 'purging most memetic effects' of individuals who use it. That counts as useable, especially to contain other SCP instances or help the Foundation in some way. I definitely think that this should remain a thaumiel class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What a curious anomaly. Notebooks that can induce memory loss and disperse the 'evaporated' memories into the air as ideas that are eventually absorbed by others.

The article kept me waiting for an explanation for why people shouldn't be forced to use it, and I wasn't disappointed. The implications of what abusing this anomaly can do took me pleasantly by surprise.

I'm a bit iffy on the Thaumiel class, though. Part of me can see why you made it Thaumiel, but I think a larger part of me thinks that this is closer to Safe.

Overall, while the anomaly is interesting and the format is good, and I kinda like it. I don't like it enough for me to upvote it. No-vote from me.

item.title: First Try
item.content:encoded: Thanks so much to ArcMage and Blibby_Blobfish for the help in making my first SCP (hopefully) a success! As a first go, I'm kind of scared, but I'm ready for it to get ripped apart. Wouldn't be able to improve otherwise!
